you to pledge generously,<|7.48|> <|8.52|> I invite you to think about your earliest memories<|13.52|> <|13.82|> of money.<|14.74|> <|16.56|> It's our earliest memories that influence us,<|20.30|> <|20.30|> the decisions we make,<|21.90|> <|21.90|> often influencing us unconsciously,<|24.78|> <|24.78|> sometimes even decades after whatever happened had happened.<|29.20|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> I definitely know that my earliest memories of money and money's meaning and power are associated with my mother, undoubtedly.<|10.70|> <|11.52|> My mother, God rest her soul, she died right at the beginning of the pandemic in January of 2020.<|18.50|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And I remember so many things about money, and especially checks, checkbooks from her.<|8.08|> <|9.72|> Our banking account was with AmSouth Bank, and I still remember exactly what the checks looked like.<|15.70|> <|16.42|> We went to a little bitty Methodist church in Guntersville, Alabama.<|22.56|> <|22.78|> Not the big first Methodist church downtown, but the little one.<|26.30|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> on the outskirts of town.<|2.00|> <|2.34|> I say we.<|3.34|> <|4.24|> My mom always made me go,<|6.88|> <|7.14|> but she and my dad never went.<|8.86|> <|11.48|> But she sent me to church<|13.66|> <|13.66|> just faithfully and dutifully.<|16.02|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And I remember that every single time that I went to church on a Sunday, every single<|5.14|> <|5.14|> time without fail, even though I had never seen her within the four walls of that church,<|11.12|> <|11.12|> she sent me with one of those Am South Bank checks to make an offering.<|16.68|> <|16.68|> Every time it was the last thing she handed me before I got out of the car, if she dropped<|21.02|> <|21.02|> me off or went out of the door, if someone else was taking me.<|24.60|> <|24.60|> And I learned an important lesson.<|26.40|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> that money is meant to be shared.<|5.00|> <|5.04|> It's not just our own.<|7.68|> <|7.68|> It comes with a moral responsibility to share with others.<|12.68|> <|14.04|> I also learned from my mom that money is associated with fear.<|18.36|> <|18.36|> Sometimes our worst fears get intertwined with money,<|22.28|> <|22.28|> how much or how little that we have.<|26.00|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> When I became a teenager, I remember that my mom started sharing just with me all of her<|7.44|> <|7.44|> anxieties over money, and that it was dwindling, and what the next few months or years would<|14.22|> <|14.22|> bring.<|15.54|> <|15.54|> That didn't exactly add up.<|17.68|> <|17.68|> She had a part-time job, but even more importantly, my dad had a good full-time job, and so I<|22.32|> <|22.32|> just couldn't quite understand as a teenager what in the world was going on with all of<|27.60|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> But what it left with me is a deep impression, and I know, and sometimes even still feel,<|6.74|> <|6.88|> how money is associated with our worst fears and anxieties.<|11.82|> <|13.98|> I also learned from my mom, and this was my favorite lesson, that money can bring great joy.<|21.56|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> great joy. I, in this little town, spent almost every day, every summer from about<|9.58|> <|9.58|> 10 years old on, at the public recreation department. Just a wonderful rec center.<|15.80|> <|15.80|> And I would go for sports, and I'd go for tennis, and I'd go for basketball, and I'd<|20.06|> <|20.06|> always go in the summer for the pool.<|22.50|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And then when I became, in 16, I was old enough to work and I became a lifeguard at that rec<|7.80|> <|7.80|> center.<|9.10|> <|9.10|> It was one of my favorite jobs I've ever had.<|11.78|> <|11.78|> I just loved it.<|13.24|> <|13.24|> And I've often wondered if that was my first calling as a preacher, actually, because a<|19.64|> <|19.64|> lifeguard stand, honestly, is a lot like a pulpit.<|23.00|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|2.24|> And, you know, you were trying to save people who didn't know how to swim, you know.<|7.60|> <|9.80|> All those little souls out there floating were sinking.<|14.50|> <|15.50|> Yeah.<|16.00|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.80|> But I just loved it.<|1.72|> <|2.52|> But every day I went there without fail,<|6.92|> <|7.00|> my mother was nothing if not ritualistic and consistent.<|11.58|> <|12.14|> And every day I went, every day,<|14.96|> <|15.62|> she would send me with a little bit of cash.<|20.80|> <|21.26|> And I vividly remember how much it was when I was in junior high.<|24.18|> <|24.18|> It was $2.25.<|25.96|> <|26.64|> It was $2.25.<|26.70|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.44|> And here's what I would do every time with that $2.25.<|4.04|> <|4.72|> I would walk across the street to, and I don't know how many people are going to know what<|9.80|> <|9.80|> I'm about to say, to the Piggly Wiggly.<|11.48|> <|12.78|> Oh, good.<|13.52|> <|14.74|> If you don't know what the Piggly Wiggly is, just substitute King Super.<|18.62|> <|18.62|> It's the same thing.<|20.40|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And I would walk across the street to the pig, and with that $2.25, I would get a sandwich<|7.28|> <|7.28|> and not just any sandwich.<|8.64|> <|8.90|> Every time I would get a chicken finger sandwich, forgive me.<|12.56|> <|13.32|> And then I would walk out of the parking lot, and I would use the rest of the money every<|18.38|> <|18.38|> single time to buy an extra large snow cone at the snow cone shack.<|23.90|> <|23.90|> Okay.<|25.10|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And it made me so happy.<|2.60|> <|3.86|> I mean, it just brought incredible joy to spend that $2.25 on all of that.<|12.22|> <|12.22|> It was just a sheer feast for a 10-year-old and a 12-year-old and a 14-year-old.<|17.82|> <|17.90|> I'm 48, and I still don't mind a snow cone on a hot day.<|20.72|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Thank you.<|1.38|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And I learned that money can bring great joy,<|5.64|> <|6.68|> and that sometimes we need to spend it,<|8.78|> <|9.10|> not just on what the preachers tell you to spend it,<|12.92|> <|12.98|> that is on your needs, but occasionally on your wants,<|16.72|> <|16.78|> because I really wanted that snow cone.<|18.52|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Amen.<|1.98|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> But what are your memories of money?<|7.00|> <|7.00|> And how does money for you get associated with fears<|13.00|> <|13.00|> and occasionally with great joys?<|16.00|> <|16.00|> Where does all of that come from<|19.00|> <|19.00|> and the power that money holds sometimes for us<|23.00|> <|23.00|> and even over us?<|27.30|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> I do ask that you pledge to, you know, in addition to all of that.<|7.72|> <|8.14|> And we ask that you pledge generously.<|10.20|> <|10.44|> There's so many great reasons.<|11.82|> <|11.82|> But here, here's what a pledge is.<|15.26|> <|15.50|> It's just simply an estimate for all that you hope to give to St. John's next year in 2023.<|22.30|> <|22.66|> It's not a contract.<|23.52|> <|23.96|> It's an estimate.<|24.96|> <|25.18|> It's an aspiration.<|26.30|> <|26.58|> We hope it's an accurate aspiration.<|28.16|> <|28.72|> It's an estimate.<|28.80|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> But it's what you hope to give throughout the course of the year, which then helps us in our vestry to make a plan and have a budget and pray thoughtfully in advance of how we're going to use your gifts.<|11.88|> <|13.90|> These are important times to pledge at St. John's.<|18.10|> <|18.20|> Our expenses are increasing, but I want to tell you they're not just increasing because of inflation.<|26.06|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> although they certainly are, as are, I know, your own household expenses.<|4.96|> <|5.38|> Our expenses are also increasing because we're growing numerically and spiritually, I hope.<|12.16|> <|12.42|> But I know we're growing numerically.<|14.04|> <|14.14|> I could give you so many examples.<|15.60|> <|16.44|> On Pentecost of this year at the 1030 service, and some of you will remember it,<|21.36|> <|21.36|> we baptized 16 people, 16 babies, 16.<|26.40|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> It was just incredible to see that many baptisms all at once.<|5.62|> <|5.62|> I tried to preach the shortest sermon I've ever preached.<|8.42|> <|11.02|> We had two weeks ago our annual parish retreat at the Diocesan Camp Cathedral Ridge.<|16.52|> <|16.62|> There were 60-something people there.<|18.10|> <|18.18|> That's a great number.<|19.20|> <|19.94|> But one quarter of them, about 15 of them, were in junior high and high school.<|25.96|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> led so gracefully and wisely by our new youth minister, Sarah Strand.<|4.82|> <|5.52|> Just incredible.<|6.50|> <|7.76|> But even more than numbers, even more than numbers,<|11.94|> <|12.62|> there's something going on spiritually, and I hope you can feel it.<|15.82|> <|15.88|> And I think part of what it is, I know it is for me,<|19.32|> <|19.52|> is that after what we've been through for three years almost now,<|25.50|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> I know I don't quite take it for granted the way that I used to sometimes on a given Sunday or on a Wednesday night.<|8.72|> <|10.12|> It's powerful to gather together in person.<|14.66|> <|14.82|> It's powerful just to look into one another's eyes.<|18.74|> <|18.84|> It's powerful to gather together in person.<|20.50|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> to listen to one another's stories.<|2.12|> <|2.24|> It's powerful to put your hand on somebody's back<|6.10|> <|6.10|> just to say thank you and keep going.<|8.38|> <|8.50|> It's just incredible to gather in this day and age<|12.58|> <|12.58|> and remember what it means to be a human being<|15.30|> <|15.30|> and to think about what it means to be loved by God<|19.00|> <|19.00|> and how to proclaim that God and that love throughout the world.<|22.74|> <|22.74|> I don't take it for granted the way I sometimes did in the past.<|27.28|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Even more, though, than what happens at services such as this or at meals and such,<|6.44|> <|6.44|> even more than that, right now, the building and grounds and our community partners,<|12.00|> <|12.26|> our community partners are asking to use the cathedral for their events,<|16.14|> <|16.26|> more so than I've ever seen in over five years.<|20.06|> <|20.10|> To give you but one of many examples I could—<|23.50|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> The city of Denver has asked us to be a voting site on November the 8th, and we will.<|6.00|> <|6.52|> And this place will just be wide open for voting.<|9.22|> <|10.04|> But there's so many other examples, and it's really important to see how these building and grounds<|16.16|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> you know, matter for this neighborhood,<|2.48|> <|2.48|> matter for this city.<|4.58|> <|4.58|> These building and grounds say something about hospitality<|7.66|> <|7.66|> and the stones cry out something about permanence<|10.48|> <|10.48|> that we are here and we're not leaving and we're committed.<|14.48|> <|14.48|> This place is not just about trafficking<|16.56|> <|16.56|> in spiritual matters.<|18.54|> <|18.54|> It really exists as well for what we used to call<|21.86|> <|21.86|> the common good, the public good for the city.<|26.50|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.96|> So for all of these reasons and so many more,<|3.36|> <|4.52|> I ask that you pledge generously.<|6.52|> <|6.96|> Know that your pledge matters.<|9.66|> <|9.90|> With God's help, your financial gifts<|12.24|> <|12.24|> are what make all of this possible.<|15.58|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> all.<|1.12|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> But above all, I hope that you know that it's your presence that matters more than anything else.<|10.10|> <|11.40|> Your stories, your hopes, your dreams, your joys, your sorrows, all of it matters deeply to St. John's Cathedral.<|24.00|> <|25.28|> And it always matters to God.<|27.90|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Thank you.<|2.86|><|endoftext|>
